questions: True ribs (ribs *1–7/1-7*) attach directly to the sternum via costal cartilages, while floating ribs (*11–12/11-12*) have free anterior ends.
The sternal angle (angle of *Louis*) is the junction of the manubrium and body of the sternum, located at the level of *T4/T4-T5*.
The intercostal neurovascular bundle runs in the costal groove in the order *VAN/Vein Artery Nerve* from superior to inferior.
The diaphragm is the primary muscle of *inspiration/respiration* and is supplied by the *phrenic* nerve (C3, C4, C5).
In a flail chest, *paradoxical* chest wall movement occurs because a segment moves independently due to multiple adjacent rib *fractures*.
